name: Ron DeSantis
summary: Ron DeSantis's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent disregard for human rights, social welfare, and democratic norms. He has supported and implemented policies that restrict access to healthcare, particularly for women and marginalized communities, such as the 15-week abortion ban and the "Don't Say Gay" bill. DeSantis has also demonstrated a willingness to suppress voting rights, limit free speech, and target minority groups, as seen in his efforts to restrict ballot initiatives, ban sanctuary cities, and transport migrants to other states. Additionally, he has been accused of using racist language and has supported legislation that discriminates against LGBTQ+ individuals, including the "Don't Say Gay" bill and the ban on transgender girls participating in sports. Overall, DeSantis's actions reveal a pattern of behavior that prioritizes partisan ideology over the well-being and dignity of marginalized communities.

entries.fact: Ron DeSantis acknowledged that he advised the commander of the Guantanamo Bay detention camp about the use of force-feeding detainees.
DeSantis signed a 'No Climate Tax Pledge' against any tax hikes to fight global warming.
DeSantis voted in favor of H.R. 45, which would have repealed the Affordable Care Act.
DeSantis introduced a bill that would have required the Justice Department to report to Congress whenever any federal agency refrained from enforcing laws.

DeSantis introduced the Let Seniors Work Act, which would have repealed an incentive to retire instead of keep working and would have exempted those above 65 from the 12.4% Social Security payroll tax.
DeSantis co-sponsored Kate's Law, which would have increased penalties for aliens who unlawfully reenter the U.S. after being removed.
DeSantis received a '0' rating from the Human Rights Campaign on LGBT-related legislation.
DeSantis voted for the 2017 Trump tax cuts.
DeSantis was present before the congressional baseball shooting and the perpetrator asked him whether the players were Republicans.
DeSantis proposed legislation that would have ended funding for the Mueller investigation of President Trump.
DeSantis opposed allowing able-bodied, childless adults to receive Medicaid.

DeSantis filed to run for the office of governor, with a platform that included support for legislation allowing people with concealed weapons permits to carry firearms openly and a state-level ban on sanctuary city protections for undocumented immigrants.
DeSantis directed Florida's elections chief to expand the availability of Spanish-language ballots and Spanish assistance for voters
DeSantis signed a measure that would make it harder to launch successful ballot initiatives
DeSantis announced his support for eliminating ballot drop boxes and limiting voting by mail, requiring voters to re-register every year to vote by mail

Ron DeSantis announced support for legislation to hold tech companies accountable for alleged political censorship, which was later blocked by a federal judge for violating the First Amendment and federal law.

Ron DeSantis signed the Fairness in Women's Sports Act, which bans transgender girls and women from participating and competing in middle-school and high-school girls' and college women's sports competitions.
DeSantis led an effort to ban the teaching of critical race theory in Florida public schools, describing it as 'teaching kids to hate their country'
The Florida Board of Education approved the ban on critical race theory, which was criticized by the Florida Education Association for trying to hide facts from students
DeSantis announced he would transport illegal immigrants to Democratic states, a decision heavily criticized by Charlie Crist as a human rights abuse.
DeSantis announced that Florida would replace the Florida Standards Assessment test with a system of three smaller tests throughout the school year
DeSantis announced a new bill, the Stop Wrongs to Our Kids and Employees Act, which would allow parents to sue school districts that teach critical race theory
Mansur Ahmad Saad al-Dayfi, a former Guantanamo detainee, alleged that Ron DeSantis oversaw the force-feeding of detainees at the Guantanamo Bay detention camp.

Ron DeSantis signed into law a bill that bans abortions after 15 weeks of pregnancy, with exceptions for physical health or fatal fetal abnormality, but no exceptions for rape, human trafficking, incest, or mental health.
Federal judge Mark E. Walker blocked enforcement of the Stop WOKE Act as applied to businesses, ruling that it violated the First Amendment and was impermissibly vague
DeSantis referred to Charlie Crist as 'the only worn-out old donkey I'm looking to put out to pastures' during a gubernatorial debate.
DeSantis previously supported raising the age to qualify for Medicare and Social Security to 70 and privatizing Medicare, but later reversed his position
Ron DeSantis signed a six-week abortion ban with exceptions for rape, incest, or human trafficking, but requires proof of a crime before permitting an abortion under these exceptions.

Ron DeSantis signed into law a bill that requires social media platforms to prohibit people under 16 years old from making accounts, which has been criticized for infringing on Floridians' First Amendment rights.
Rep. DeSantis issued a statement in support of repealing ObamaCare
Ron DeSantis condemned the Venezuelan protest crackdowns
Ron DeSantis joined Marco Rubio to introduce the 'Let Seniors Work Act'
DeSantis signed an anti-'sanctuary city' bill into law, requiring local governments to aid federal immigration-enforcement efforts
DeSantis signed a bill requiring government employers and contractors to use E-Verify
DeSantis halted cooperation with the Biden administration's program to relocate and resettle migrants in Florida
DeSantis's administration allocated $12 million for relocating migrants to other states
DeSantis signed into law the Combating Public Disorder Act, which forbade intimidation by mobs and penalized damage to historic properties or memorials
DeSantis introduced a $5,000 signing bonus for Florida police officers in a bid to attract out-of-state police recruits
DeSantis signed a bill creating an election police unit to investigate election fraud
DeSantis voiced support for the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act, which prohibits discussion of sexual orientation or gender identity in school classrooms from kindergarten to grade 3
DeSantis signed the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act into law, which took effect on July 1, 2022
DeSantis signed a bill eliminating Disney's special independent district act and replacing its Disney-appointed board of overseers
The state board of education extended the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act's restrictions on classroom instruction to grades 4-12
Disney filed suit against DeSantis and several others, accusing them of retaliating against protected speech

DeSantis recruited 50 newly arrived asylum seekers and flew them to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts, where they did not debark, in an action that was later described as 'extreme and outrageous, and utterly intolerable in a civilized community'
DeSantis questioned climate science, supported fossil fuels, opposed renewables, and sanctioned firms for considering environmental issues in their investments
DeSantis refused to accept $346 million from the Inflation Reduction Act for rebates to homeowners who want to retrofit their houses and make it more energy efficient
Ron DeSantis saw force-feeding of hunger strikers while serving at Guantanamo Bay, a practice characterized as torture by the United Nations
DeSantis announced plans to send over 1,000 personnel to Texas to help stem the influx of illegal immigration across the southern border
Ron DeSantis was accused of overselling his Navy SEAL career at a GOP debate
DeSantis vetoed a bill that would have created a statewide process to issue closures and send warnings if the bacteria in waterways reached unsafe levels
DeSantis and his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier, were involved in a controversy over the transfer of $10,000,000 from a Medicaid settlement to a political committee Uthmeier controlled
State Attorney Jack Campbell opened a criminal investigation into allegations of money laundering and wire fraud involving Ron DeSantis's chief of staff, Uthmeier
DeSantis sidelined scientists and followed Trump's lead in responding to the COVID-19 pandemic
DeSantis announced legislation to crack down on big tech and online censorship
DeSantis vowed to punish big tech for targeting conservatives
Florida joined states to ban transgender girls from sports
DeSantis signed the 'Don't Say Gay' bill into law
A court case, Michael Bernard Bell v. State of Florida, was filed against the State of Florida under DeSantis' leadership
A court case, Edward J. Zakrzewski, II v. State of Florida, was filed against the State of Florida under DeSantis' leadership
A court case, Bailey v. Laurie, was filed against DeSantis
Ron DeSantis, as part of the State of Florida, was a defendant in the Kayle B. Bates v. State of Florida court case.
Ron DeSantis, as part of the State of Florida, was a defendant in the Victor Tony Jones v. State of Florida court case.
Ron DeSantis was a defendant in the McGraw v. DeSantis court case.
Governor Ron DeSantis was named as a defendant in the lawsuit Maurice Jackson W v. Ron Desantis
Governor Ron DeSantis was named as a defendant in the lawsuit Laurie N. Reynolds v. Judge John M. Alexander, et al.
The Secretary, Department of Corrections, under Ron DeSantis' governance, was a defendant in the lawsuit Frank A. Walls v. Secretary, Department of Corrections.
DeSantis proposed slashing tolls for Floridians, shifting costs to out-of-state residents, potentially disproportionately affecting tourists and snowbirds.
Ron DeSantis announced over 10,400 immigration arrests in Florida as part of Operation Tidal Wave, a crackdown on immigration.
Ron DeSantis referred to a San Francisco Reparations Committee's 100-point plan as 'the woke mind virus'.
DeSantis called a special session to redraw Florida congressional maps
A federal trial began in Miami regarding a redistricting lawsuit related to Governor Ron DeSantis
The Florida Bar appealed a discipline report for a lawyer who sued Governor Ron DeSantis
Florida Governor Ron DeSantis proposed a ban on H-1B visas at public universities in the state.
Ron DeSantis, as Governor, was a defendant in the lawsuit Ronald Palmer Heath v. State of Florida.
Ron DeSantis, as Governor, was a defendant in the lawsuit Gilberti v. Desantis, Governor, Biden.
Florida voters sued over DeSantis' mid-decade redistricting
Florida, under Ron DeSantis, moved to implement English-only driver's license exams after a deadly truck crash.
DeSantis' immigration crackdown has been criticized for being costly, with some arguing it duplicates existing efforts.
A federal judge blocked Florida Governor Ron DeSantis' attempt to label certain Muslim groups as foreign terrorists.
